subject: Geography
title: São Paulo: South America's Engine
description: A fast breakdown of São Paulo: South America's Engine for builders.

hook.text: São Paulo's metro area holds 22 million people—the largest city in the Southern Hemisphere.

buildup.text: Coffee wealth built the city in the 1800s. Industry and finance kept it growing.

discovery.text: It produces a third of Brazil's GDP and is home to the largest Japanese community outside Japan.

twist.text: In 2015, a drought nearly emptied the city's reservoirs. Taps ran dry across neighborhoods.

climax.text: A megacity surrounded by rainforest came within weeks of running out of water.

punchline.text: Near the world's largest river basin, a city almost died of thirst.

quiz.question: What crisis did São Paulo face in 2015?
